title: FWM Product Category Staging And Manual Review

metadata.reason: Desktop thread full transcript export was not available; this focused file captures the product category staging schema work, manual review application, Supabase updates, and git handoff.

messages.text: Asked to find and open the planning file for updating fashion item ID information based on product pages.
Located the product category planning document and used it as the working plan for staging product-page-based category data.
Asked for frontend search UX ideas for the future implementation, especially dropdown versus autocomplete for clothing types.
Proposed several frontend search experiences. The selected future direction was a grouped hybrid combo box/typeahead, but the user explicitly deferred implementing any web experience changes.

Asked to start implementation without changing the web experience and without connecting the live website to new tables; requested new schema using staging tables populated from existing product links in public.images.

Created staging migrations for private product category tables and functions. Added staging.clothing_mother_categories, staging.clothing_type_tags, staging.product_pages, staging.product_page_clothing_type_tags, staging.product_page_image_sources, staging.normalize_product_url, staging.category_from_product_signal, and staging.refresh_product_category_staging. Populated the staging tables from public.images product links.

Asked for a Supabase URL to inspect the new tables, then reported problems: categories should never be other, empty columns needed filling, brand values like WWW were incorrect, and some rows were marked for manual review.

Added quality migrations to improve classification, avoid other as a clothing category, correct slug classification, patch remaining staging categories, classify unavailable pages as source-review, and populate blank product titles. Added scripts/enrich-staging-product-pages.mjs for CLI-based product page enrichment because the staging schema is not exposed through PostgREST.

Asked for rows needing manual review to be exported into an Excel sheet with product title, catalog photo URL, customer review image URL, product URL, a 0/1 correctness column, and a brief review reason.
Generated a manual review workbook at /Users/briannasinger/Projects/FWM/FWM_Data/_reports/product_category_manual_review_2026-05-20.xlsx.

Returned a filled CSV. Clarified that 0 in new_category_correct_0_or_1 means the old observed category is correct; 1 means the new category is correct; product not available rows should be added to image_reports so they are removed from the website. Also noted tag suggestions must agree and should not mix incompatible garment types such as dress and jeans.

Applied manual review decisions with migration 20260520060000_apply_product_category_manual_review_decisions.sql. For 0 decisions, accepted the old observed clothing type and reset tags to coherent accepted-category tags. For 1 decisions, accepted the proposed category. For product no longer available decisions, set staging rows to source-review and inserted public.image_reports rows with reason dead_link and anon_id manual_product_category_review_2026_05_20. Added 34 dead-link reports.

Added migration 20260520070000_hide_manual_dead_link_reports_from_search.sql to update public.match_by_measurements so those manual dead_link reports are excluded from live search results. This was a narrow live-search filter only for the manual review job, not a frontend category/search redesign.
Apologized and provided an updated CSV with the previously blank row filled.

Applied migration 20260520080000_apply_remaining_manual_review_decision.sql for the final filled row, White Short Sleeve Shirt. Set mother_category_id to tops, product_category_raw to shirt, tags to shirt and tops, and needs_manual_review to false. Archived the updated CSV to /Users/briannasinger/Projects/FWM/FWM_Data/_reports/product_category_manual_review_completed_2026-05-20_v2.csv.
Asked what still needed manual review and where the 19 came from.

Clarified that the count of 19 came from staging.product_pages where needs_manual_review = true, but those are not remaining category decisions. They are the product-unavailable/source-review rows where the user wrote product no longer available. They already generated 34 public.image_reports rows with reason dead_link.
Asked to update the remote git and the transcripts table.

Prepared this focused transcript, planned to upload it to the Supabase codex_chat_transcripts table, and planned to commit and push only the product category staging/manual-review changes while leaving unrelated dirty worktree files untouched.

verification: Supabase migrations through 20260520080000 were pushed to the remote project.
Final formerly blank row was verified with mother_category_id=tops, product_category_raw=shirt, tags=[shirt,tops], and needs_manual_review=false.
Manual dead-link image reports count was verified as 34.
Remaining needs_manual_review rows were verified as 19 source-review/product-unavailable rows, not pending category decisions.
Reviewed mixed dress/jeans tag rows were verified as 0 before the final row correction.
next_steps: Do not connect the live website to staging category tables until the user explicitly asks for the web experience change.
When ready for frontend work, build the grouped hybrid category combo box/typeahead described in product-category-tags-plan.md.
Treat source-review rows as dead/unavailable product-page cases rather than garment category review tasks.
